weakest_union: The term 'Representatives' is used in Section 3 without any definition, creating an internal gap and undermining structural coherence.
The definition of 'Purpose' is embedded within the 'Confidential Information' definition in Section 1, disrupting the logical organization of defined terms.

Section 4 combines a positive duty of care with a limitation-on-liability phrase ('will not be responsible for inadvertent disclosures absent gross negligence') in a single sentence, resulting in slight drafting awkwardness.

Section 6 Exclusions uses vague, non-standard phrasing ('reasonably understands was already known', 'becomes available from any source', 'can be used in Recipient's ordinary business without reference') that undermines clarity and professional register.
Section 3 uses the undefined term 'Representatives', creating structural incoherence and ambiguity.
Section 8 Return or Destruction carves out 'ordinary business recordkeeping' without defining scope or continuing obligations, creating internal tension with the obligation to return or destroy.

Section 6's exclusion language is unusually vague and not drafted in standard legal prose; 'reasonably understands was already known' and 'becomes available from any source' lack necessary qualifiers, introducing ambiguity and drafting-register inconsistency.
